title: NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Operational Signals

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(e) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) (Security in acquisition, development and maintenance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.27: NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Operational Signals.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.27' running on index=itsm (vuln disclosure tickets), index=email_gateway (parsed security@), HEC from bug bounty APIs, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:incident` (number, category, opened_at, work_start, state) ingested via Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) using `/now/table/incident`; `index=email_gateway` `sourcetype=ms:o365:message` or `sourcetype=proofpoint:tap` for messages sent to `security@`; HEC JSON from HackerOne `reports` API (resource `/v1/reports`) landing in `index=bugbounty`.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)), HTTP Event Collector global token for **bug bounty** and **secure mailbox** JSON.
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" category="*Vulnerability Disclosure*" earliest=-90d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval triaged=strptime(work_start,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval triage_hours=round((triaged-opened)/3600,2)
| eval sla_breach=if(triage_hours>48 OR isnull(triaged),1,0)
| stats count as tickets sum(sla_breach) as breaches avg(triage_hours) as avg_triage
| eval breach_pct=round(100*breaches/tickets,1)
| table tickets, breaches, breach_pct, avg_triage
description: Aggregates ServiceNow incidents tagged for coordinated disclosure and calculates what share missed a 48-hour work-start SLA, producing breach rate and average triage hours for leadership review.
value: Researchers and regulators increasingly ask for proof you respond quickly; this search turns mailbox chaos into two numbers—percent late and average hours—suitable for an annual assurance letter without hand-counting tickets.
implementation: (1) Normalize `category` values in SNOW so disclosure incidents always match `*Vulnerability Disclosure*`. (2) Map `work_start` or `work_notes` first activity timestamp consistently — adjust `strptime` format to your locale. (3) Use HEC Ack for bug bounty ingestion at `/services/collector/event`. (4) Keep raw email in `index=email_gateway` with PII access restricted via RBAC. (5) Export monthly PDF to `index=audit_evidence`.
visualization: Single value breach_pct, line chart avg_triage, table of worst incidents with drilldown to SNOW.

references.title: ENISA Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure — Good practices

knownFalsePositives:
• **Major Sev-1 outage steals triage time** — Distinguish: incidents opened during documented crisis window with problem record. Suppress: `crisis_blackout.csv` time-bound exception register matched on opened_at.
• **Holidays or on-call rotation gaps** — Distinguish: no analyst logged `work_start` until Tuesday. Suppress: adjust SLA macro for bank holidays via lookup `holiday_calendar.csv`.
• **Duplicate SNOW records for same researcher email** — Distinguish: two incidents share external reporter hash. Suppress: `dedup number` after sort -_time or merge workflow in ServiceNow.
• **Bugcrowd API lag replays old reports** — Distinguish: `_time` cluster but `opened_at` stale. Suppress: filter HEC events `where payload_age_seconds < 900` at ingest time using **props.conf** `EVAL`.
• **Category typo breaks match** — Distinguish: team renamed category to `Vuln Disclosure`. Suppress: broaden search `where like(category,"%Disclosure%")` or maintain `nis2_vd_categories.csv` lookup.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) with service account using `restapi` role; OAuth token stored in **passwords.conf** via Manager UI.
• Search head must reach `https://<instance>.service-now.com/api/now/table/incident` — allow firewall **443/tcp** outbound.
• Incident field extraction: confirm `category`, `opened_at`, `work_start` exist; optional use `sys_updated_on` if work_start unreliable — document mapping in runbook.
• HEC token with index=`bugbounty` sourcetype=`hackerone:report` for bounty feeds; enable indexer acknowledgement if WAN is lossy.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Under **Inputs → ServiceNow** enable incremental incident poller every **300 seconds** with filter `sysparm_query=categorySTARTSWITHVulnerability Disclosure` to reduce API volume.
For secure mailboxes, use Splunk Connect for O365 or Proofpoint TA, route to `index=email_gateway`, mask PII at index if required by DPO.
Configure **HTTP Event Collector** global settings → **Enable SSL** on port **8088** when receiving HackerOne webhooks.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run SPL monthly first (`earliest=-90d@d`) to baseline breach_pct. Save as `UC-22.2.27_vd_sla`.
Alert when `breach_pct > 15` **OR** `avg_triage > 36` hours — tune for your policy (48h is default threshold inside eval).
Add `appendcols` subsearch from `index=bugbounty` if you need researcher-facing SLAs separate from SNOW.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick five incidents in ServiceNow UI, compare `opened_at` vs first work note timestamp manually; ensure delta matches `triage_hours` within rounding.
Use `| fillnull value=0 sla_breach` before stats to treat untriaged as breach (matches eval).
Validate REST input vs UI counts: `| tstats count where index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category="*Vulnerability Disclosure*"` should approximate SNOW list length.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard tab 1: KPI tiles. Tab 2: histogram of triage_hours. Tab 3: hyperlink to `https://<instance>.service-now.com/nav_to.do?uri=incident` with tokenized `number`.
Quarterly report: PDF + CSV to `index=audit_evidence` with `_signTime` if using Splunk ACS export tools.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **strptime returns null** — timestamp strings include timezone suffix; extend format to `%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S%Z`.
• **work_start always null** — field not mirrored from SNOW; switch to `u_first_response` custom column via field alias.
• **Zero tickets** — ServiceNow filter too strict; loosen `category` OR fix case sensitivity with lower().
• **Duplicate counting** — incidents reopened; add `| dedup number sortby - _time` before stats.
• **HEC 400 errors** — JSON not wrapped in `{\"event\": {...}}`; fix HEC sender template.
grandmaExplanation: We measure how quickly real people answer when someone emails a security weakness, so you can show the world you treat those messages seriously instead of letting them gather dust.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel/competent-authority review.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.27` monthly workbook, ServiceNow incident export hash, and HEC receipt IDs archived under the vulnerability disclosure control folder.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Import fixture `snow:incident` rows where half the tickets never receive `work_start` within 48 hours of `opened_at`; the summary panel shows `breach_pct` above zero and `avg_triage` above the policy line.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Replay fixtures where every incident records `work_start` within six hours and categories strictly follow the disclosure naming standard; the search shows `breach_pct` equal to zero while ticket volume remains non-zero.
